clinical_history: 40 year-old man seen in the emergency ward with complaint of chest pain.  The ECG is most consistent with which one of the following diagnoses?
diagnosis: The ECG shows sinus rhythm with slight J point and ST segment elevations most apparent in the mid-precordial leads consistent with the early repolarization variant.  There is also very slight notching of the terminal QRS (V4) which is often seen in conjunction with this physiologic pattern. (For a detailed explanation and comparison see Case #130.)  Note: A normal ECG does not exclude myocardial ischemia. A detailed history and physical examination suggested non-cardiac pain. Relevant laboratory tests, including cardiac enzymes, were within the normal limits.  Diagnoses of gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) and musculo-skeletal pain were made.
